generated_comment: Yaar from company side bhi dekho na, if they start separate period leave and then maternity leave bhi itna long, obviously project planning thoda affect hoga. Fairness ka bhi point hai — boys ke liye aisa koi buffer nahi hota, appraisal time pe phir same expectations kaise rahengi?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s biological and reproductive needs as an organizational burden and as unfair special treatment compared with men. It encodes fairness/reverse-discrimination framing and productivity-cost concerns around menstrual and maternity leave.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 24. Single. A college student in Chandigarh. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone just shared a photo from last night's college fest where several female students are dancing in western outfits. You are reacting to the photo and commenting on the girls in the picture and what you think about girls who dress and dance like that at college events and what kind of girls they are.
